payload.message: Read through today's memory files and update MEMORY.md with distilled learnings. Remove outdated info from MEMORY.md that's no longer relevant.

SILENT TASK — DO NOT send any message unless instructed below.

Check: ls "/data/obsidian/To claw/Bot/crash-configs/" 2>/dev/null

If the folder is empty or does not exist — STOP IMMEDIATELY. Output nothing. No explanation. No confirmation. Absolute silence.

Only if .md files exist: read each file, analyze the git diff, append a lesson to "/data/obsidian/To claw/Bot/lessons-learned.md" (format: ## DATE — title / What happened / Root cause / Rule), delete the crash file, then send ONE Telegram notification via sysadmin agent.

Scan the Obsidian vault at /data/obsidian for all unchecked tasks (lines matching `- [ ]`). Use bash: grep -r '- \[ \]' /data/obsidian --include='*.md' -l to find files, then read the relevant ones. Group tasks by project/folder. Send a concise morning briefing to Telegram: what needs attention today. Prioritize tasks from '01_Projects' and 'To claw' folders. Skip tasks in Archive or Templates folders. Keep the message compact.

Run a system health check and send a status dashboard to Telegram. Check: (1) Docker containers — run `docker ps --format 'table {{.Names}}\t{{.Status}}\t{{.RunningFor}}'` via bash tool; (2) Disk usage — `df -h /`; (3) Any recent entries in `/data/obsidian/To claw/Bot/lessons-learned.md` (last 3). Format as a clean status report with emoji indicators: ✅ healthy, ⚠️ warning, ❌ issue.

SILENT TASK — only send to Telegram if issues are found.

Scan /data/obsidian/To claw/ for quality issues:
1. Empty files: bash: find '/data/obsidian/To claw' -name '*.md' -empty
2. Broken wikilinks: grep for [[links]] patterns and verify the target file exists in the vault

If issues found: send a brief summary to Telegram with file paths and issue types. If nothing found: complete silently with no output.
Summarize what was done today based on memory files and current status of projects. Report any issues.
